metadata.title: csv export drops body (no --include-body) and uses nonexistent due_date/milestone create flags
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Added --include-body to export; switched to deadline column mapped to --deadline; dropped invalid --milestone flag. Round-trip verified.

issues/pm-csv-l4vy.toon.body:
Data-loss / wrong field mapping. (a) export ran `list-all --json` WITHOUT --include-body, so the body column was ALWAYS empty. (b) export columns and import flags used due_date and milestone, which do not exist in pm — `pm create` has --deadline and no milestone. So every imported row carrying due_date/milestone FAILED with "Unknown option --milestone", and export never populated those columns.

Repro (before fix):
  export of an item with body -> body column empty.
  pm csv import md.csv (md.csv has due_date,milestone) -> "Row 2: create failed — Unknown option --milestone", imported 0 skipped 1, exit 0.
Expected: body round-trips; deadline round-trips.

Fix: export now passes --include-body; replaced due_date/milestone columns with a single `deadline` column (still accepts a legacy due_date header on import) mapped to `--deadline`; removed the invalid --milestone create flag. Verified: body + deadline both survive a full export->import round-trip.

metadata.title: Hands-on functional test pass 2026-05-29 (real data)
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: All three bugs fixed, rebuilt, re-tested green. pm-csv functional pass complete.

features/pm-csv-z5f8.toon.body: Functional test pass running every pm-csv command against a real pm workspace with seeded items. Verifying exit codes and that flags take effect. Hunting the 4 known bug classes.

metadata.title: Add bun-install verification to release workflow
metadata.description: Smoke-test that the just-published npm package installs cleanly via bun via daily release workflow.
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: Release readiness hardening for pm-csv
metadata.description: Prepare pm-csv as a production-ready pm package with package-local pm tracking, generated changelog data, CI and daily release automation.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.acceptance_criteria: Package installs with pm install; npm release gate passes; changelog is generated from package-local pm data; tracked files and reachable git history have no secret pattern hits.

tasks/pm-csv-1pty.toon.body: Implemented release-ready package metadata and automation for pm-csv using the latest pm CLI 2026.5.24 and pm SDK contracts. Added local pm project tracking, CI/release workflows, pm-changelog integration, TypeScript checks, production audit gates, dry-run packaging, installability metadata, and registry metadata. Verified no private tokens or personal files are present in tracked files or reachable git history before release preparation.

metadata.title: csv import/export return error object instead of throwing (exit 0 on failure)
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Replaced return {error} with throw in import+export handlers. Verified missing-file/no-title/no-arg all exit 1.

issues/pm-csv-e9v4.toon.body:
Bug class #4 (wrong exit code on failure). Error paths returned { error } which the host treats as success (exit 0). A scripted pipeline could not detect failures.

Repro (before fix):
  pm csv import /tmp/nope.csv ; echo $?   -> 0  (missing file)
  pm csv import notitle.csv ; echo $?      -> 0  (no title column)
  pm csv import ; echo $?                  -> 0  (no arg)
Expected: non-zero.

Fix: replaced return { error } with throw new Error(...) in the import handler (no-arg, read failure, missing title column) and the export handler (list-all failure). Verified all three now exit 1; export-to-stdout still exits 0.

metadata.title: csv import --dry-run silently ignored (still writes)
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Fixed via readBoolOption helper reading both dry-run and dryRun. Verified dry-run writes nothing (before=3 after=3).

issues/pm-csv-zw3a.toon.body:
Bug class #1 (option-key normalization). The runtime normalizes --dry-run to camelCase dryRun, but both the `csv import` command handler and the `csv-import` importer read ctx.options["dry-run"], which is always undefined.

Repro:
  pm csv import export.csv --dry-run
Observed: actually imported 5 items (before=5 after=10), output "Imported 5 item(s)". Exit 0.
Expected: write nothing, print "[dry-run] Would import...".

Fix: added readBoolOption(options, "dry-run", "dryRun") helper and used it. Verified: before=3 after=3, prints dry-run preview.

metadata.title: Add publish retry + provenance fallback to release workflow
metadata.description: Replace the npm publish step in .github/workflows/release.yml with a resilient version that (1) skips if the version is already published, (2) retries up to 3 times with 30s sleep, and (3) falls back to publishing without --provenance after persistent registry 404s. Permanent fix for the 2026-05-27 npm E404 outage that left all 19 packages tagged in git but unpublished on npm.
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: ci: fix release workflow step ordering
metadata.description: Swap changelog regeneration before release:check so changelog:check sees the version-bumped CHANGELOG.md instead of failing the gate.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Swapped changelog generation before release checks so changelog:check sees updated file. Verified workflow dispatch then triggers npm publish + GH release.
